title: Pareto Analysis: Find the Vital Few in Your Data
summary: Most revenue often comes from a minority of customers or SKUs. Pareto thinking focuses effort.

content.summary: Most revenue often comes from a minority of customers or SKUs. Pareto thinking focuses effort.

body.text: The eighty-twenty rule is a heuristic, not a law—but it prioritizes where to look first. Bar chart categories sorted descending by revenue. Note cumulative share at top five, top ten. Filter out noise categories with saved rules before ranking.

body.alt: Team collaborating around data on a screen
body.caption: Team collaborating around data on a screen. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Bar chart categories sorted descending by revenue. Note cumulative share at top five, top ten.
Filter out noise categories with saved rules before ranking.

items: Combine long tail only when finance allows.
Re-run monthly—vital few shifts with product mix.

body.text: Account teams focus on top cohort; ops fixes issues in high-volume small-deal segments separately.
Document which slice the Pareto chart uses.
Datilio sorted bar charts make Pareto passes a five-minute Monday habit.

meta_description: Most revenue often comes from a minority of customers or SKUs. Pareto thinking focuses effort.
